summary: Added a repository-root `gen.sh` helper for generating M2C-derived C stubs from asm files. The script can regenerate `include/ctx_includes.h`, regenerate `src/ctx.c` via `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py`, and emit `.s.c` files under matching `src/` paths for asm inputs, with optional path filtering, console clear, and log teeing. Supporting changes made generated outputs ignored by git, changed `m2ctx.py` to print context output instead of writing `ctx.c` directly, adjusted a few headers so they work better in generated context/include aggregation, and fixed `build.sh` expected-sync by ensuring `build` and `expected/build` directories exist.
what_changed: Created `gen.sh`, a 95-line shell script that can generate `ctx_includes.h`, generate `ctx.c`, and run `tools/m2c/m2c.py` over asm files to produce generated C files.
`gen.sh` maps asm paths to src paths with `.c` appended, so an asm file becomes a generated `.s.c` file under the corresponding `src` directory.

`gen.sh` supports `-i` for include aggregation, `-c` for context generation, `-g` for M2C generation, `-p pattern` for restricting asm paths, `-x` for clearing the console, `-l` for teeing output to `gen.log`, and `-h` for usage help.

`gen.sh` generates `include/ctx_includes.h` by finding headers under `include/` and `src/`, converting them to angle-bracket `#include <...>` lines, excluding the generated `ctx_includes.h` itself, and appending `tools/m2c/m2c_macros.h`.
`tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` was changed from writing `ctx.c` at the repository root to printing generated context output to stdout, letting `gen.sh` decide the final output path `src/ctx.c`.

`.gitignore` now groups generated files and ignores `ctx.c`, `ctx_includes.h`, `*.s.c`, and `*.m2c`; `ctx.c` and `ctx_includes.h` were moved from the generic 'Other' section to the generated-file section.
`build.sh` now creates `build` and `expected/build` before rsyncing build output to expected output.

`src/melee/mp/mpcoll.h` changed includes from quoted local includes to angle-bracket project includes, replacing `global.h`, `types.h`, and `melee/ft/fighter.h` with `<dolphin/types.h>` and `<melee/ft/fighter.h>`.

Several extern declarations were removed from generated/unknown GX headers, including `__GXContext`, `__GXDefaultTlutRegionCallback`, `__GXDefaultTexRegionCallback`, `GXSetTlutRegionCallback`, and `GXSetTexRegionCallback`.
smart_moves: Centralized a repeated decomp workflow into a single script: building include context, building M2C context, and generating candidate C from asm can now be run with one command such as `./gen.sh -cig`.
Kept generated M2C outputs out of version control by adding `*.s.c`, `ctx.c`, and `ctx_includes.h` to `.gitignore`.
Made `m2ctx.py` more composable by printing to stdout rather than hardcoding an output file, which allows callers like `gen.sh` to choose where the generated context belongs.
Added path-pattern filtering so contributors can generate all asm, a subsystem such as `gx` or `ft/`, or one specific file without editing the script.
Ensured generated C starts with `#include <ctx_includes.h>`, making each generated file use the same consolidated M2C context.
Used null-delimited `find ... -print0` with `read -r -d ''` for safer path handling during asm traversal.
Created destination directories before writing generated `.s.c` files, avoiding manual setup for nested asm/src path mirrors.
Fixed a build-sync edge case by creating `build` and `expected/build` before `rsync`.
decomp_lessons: For M2C workflows, keep generated context and generated `.s.c` files ignored by git; they are useful local artifacts but should not pollute decomp source history.
A context-generation tool is easier to reuse when it writes to stdout and lets wrappers control file placement.
When auto-aggregating headers into a single context include file, exclude the generated aggregate itself to avoid recursive inclusion.

Generated M2C C files can be organized by mirroring the asm tree into `src/` and using a distinct `.s.c` suffix so contributors can inspect generated candidates without confusing them for matched source.

Header include style matters for generated contexts: angle-bracket project paths such as `<dolphin/types.h>` and `<melee/ft/fighter.h>` are more suitable for aggregated include generation than ambiguous quoted relative includes.

Some declarations in ad hoc or unknown headers may conflict with context generation and should be removed or normalized when building an all-header M2C context, but this PR does not document the exact conflict mechanism.
naming_conventions: Generated M2C output files use the `.s.c` suffix.
The aggregate include file is named `ctx_includes.h` and lives under `include/` when generated by `gen.sh`.
The M2C context file is named `ctx.c` and is written under `src/` by `gen.sh`.
Generated files are grouped under a `# Generated` section in `.gitignore`.
Project includes used for context generation favor angle-bracket paths like `<dolphin/types.h>` and `<melee/ft/fighter.h>`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `./gen.sh -cig` to regenerate `include/ctx_includes.h`, regenerate `src/ctx.c`, and generate `.s.c` files for all asm files.
Use `./gen.sh -cigl` to do the same generation while teeing output to `gen.log`.
Use `./gen.sh -cigl -p gx` to restrict generated files to paths matching `gx`.
Use `./gen.sh -cigl -p ft/` to restrict generation to the `ft` directory.
Use `./gen.sh -cigl -p code_8007C114` to generate for a specific asm file name pattern.
The M2C invocation added by the script is `python tools/m2c/m2c.py -t ppc-mwcc-c --valid-syntax --context ./src/ctx.c <asm file>`.
The script prepends `#include <ctx_includes.h>` to each generated output before appending M2C output.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify or replace `gen.sh`.
Search for changes to `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` after it began printing context output to stdout.
Look for later cleanup of generated `.s.c` workflows or changes to ignored generated files.
Search for follow-up fixes involving `ctx_includes.h`, `src/ctx.c`, or M2C context-generation failures.
Inspect later header normalization PRs that convert quoted includes to angle-bracket project includes.
